required_header.download_date: ClinicalTrials.gov processed this data on February 2026
required_header.link_text: Link to the current ClinicalTrials.gov record.

clinical_study.brief_title: Osimertinib in Metastatic EGFR-Mutant Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer with Brain Metastases

brief_summary.textblock: This study evaluates the efficacy and safety of osimertinib (80 mg daily) as first-line therapy in patients with Stage IV non-small cell lung cancer harboring EGFR exon 19 deletion or exon 21 L858R mutation, including patients with stable brain metastases at baseline. The primary endpoint is progression-free survival per RECIST 1.1.

detailed_description.textblock: Patients with metastatic NSCLC (adenocarcinoma histology) and a sensitizing EGFR mutation (exon 19 deletion or exon 21 L858R) detected by liquid biopsy or tumor tissue NGS are eligible. Patients with stable, previously untreated or previously treated brain metastases are permitted if neurologically stable and not requiring escalating doses of steroids. Patients receive osimertinib 80 mg orally once daily until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity. Response assessment by CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and brain MRI every 12 weeks. The study specifically includes patients with prior smoking history and mild to moderate COPD if lung function allows adequate performance status.

clinical_study.study_design: Intervention Model: Single Group Assignment, Primary Purpose: Treatment

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria:
- Histologically confirmed non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma histology
- Stage IV (metastatic) disease, including M1b (single extrathoracic metastasis) or M1c (multiple extrathoracic metastases)
- EGFR activating mutation: exon 19 deletion or exon 21 L858R, confirmed by liquid biopsy (ctDNA) or tumor tissue molecular testing
- Brain metastases permitted if clinically stable (no neurologic progression, no increasing steroid requirement over the past 2 weeks)
- Age 18 years or older
- ECOG performance status 0 or 1
- Adequate organ function: ANC greater than or equal to 1.5, platelets greater than or equal to 100, creatinine less than or equal to 1.5x ULN, AST and ALT less than or equal to 3x ULN (or 5x ULN if liver metastases present)
- Prior smoking history (including former smokers) permitted
- Mild to moderate COPD permitted if ECOG PS 0-1
Exclusion Criteria:
- ALK rearrangement or ROS1 rearrangement as primary oncogenic driver
- KRAS mutation present
- Prior systemic therapy for metastatic NSCLC (treatment-naive patients only)
- Prior EGFR-targeted therapy of any kind
- Leptomeningeal disease
- Active interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring treatment
- Uncontrolled pleural effusion requiring repeated drainage
- QTc interval greater than 470 ms at baseline
- Active current smoker with more than 10 cigarettes per day
